definition: The 1942-1951 Treasury-Fed Accord analogue substantially overstates the ease of fiscal dominance resolution because three critical features present post-WWII are absent today: labor force growth (~1.5% vs ~0.3%), pent-up consumer demand from wartime rationing, and Bretton Woods capital controls preventing arbitrage of repressed rates. Post-WWII r-g was -3 to -5pp; current r-g is approximately zero.
evidence: Solow-Swan decomposition reveals potential GDP growth gap of 2.5-3.0pp
Post-WWII r-g differential was -3 to -5pp vs approximately zero today
Financial repression must operate covertly through regulatory captive buying rather than overt rate caps
Analogue provides directional guidance but magnitudes are not replicable
open_questions: Could regulatory captive buying (LCR, RBC) achieve comparable repression to overt rate caps?
How does the absence of capital controls change the sustainability of financial repression?
What r-g differential is achievable through stealth repression in current conditions?
